title: bilabrella woodii
intro: Bilabrella woodii, a flowering plant native to Australia, is a member of the Rutaceae family and is also known as the 'Australian caper' due to its edible flower buds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Bilabrella woodii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are used in teas, infusions, and capsules to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
